message:
feat(core,mvcc,wal,obs): expand connection layer, SSI conflict model, WAL FEC pipeline tests, and observability hooks

The connection layer in fsqlite-core grows by ~800 lines to support the full
SQLite connection lifecycle: pragma negotiation, busy-handler configuration,
journal-mode switching, and WAL checkpoint coordination. This is the
foundational wiring that every higher-level parity orchestrator will exercise
when it opens a real database handle.

The MVCC conflict model gains a 216-line SSI (Serializable Snapshot Isolation)
validation path. SSI is the mechanism that detects dangerous read-write
dependency cycles between concurrent transactions and aborts the offending
one. Without this, FrankenSQLite cannot claim serializable isolation for its
MVCC layer. The lifecycle module expands by 172 lines to manage transaction
begin/commit/rollback state transitions and integrate with the new SSI
validator.

A 91-line benchmark suite is added for MVCC so we can track conflict-detection
latency as the dependency graph grows. Regressions in SSI validation speed
would directly impact write throughput under contention.

The WAL FEC pipeline test (122 lines) exercises the RaptorQ forward-error-
correction path end-to-end: encode WAL frames, corrupt a subset, and verify
the decoder recovers the original payload. This guards against silent data
loss on unreliable storage.

The observability crate gains 109 lines of structured metrics and tracing
hooks so every subsystem above can emit counters, histograms, and spans
through a single facade.

The top-level lib.rs and Cargo.lock are updated to wire the new modules into
the workspace.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
